name: KYC Analyst (Enhanced Due Diligence)
purpose: Conducts enhanced due diligence on high-risk customers to ensure the bank has a complete and sound understanding of the customer's identity, business, source of funds, and the actual risk associated with the customer. Critical control function preventing the bank from establishing or maintaining customer relationships linked to money laundering, terrorist financing, or other financial crime.
responsibilities: Conduct enhanced due diligence (EDD) for higher-risk customers
Review and validate customer ownership, control, and corporate/organisational structure
Assess source of funds and source of wealth where required
Perform periodic KYC reviews based on risk level
Identify discrepancies between customer information and observed behaviour
Document risk assessments and conclusions in an auditable and regulator-ready manner
Escalate suspicious indicators to AML investigations as needed
key_risk_themes: Beneficial ownership and control
High-risk customer onboarding
Ongoing customer due diligence and periodic reviews
Jurisdictional and geographic risk
Documentation and audit trail
Escalation and reporting

knowledge: Enhanced due diligence (EDD) requirements
Ownership and control structures
Source of funds and source of wealth concepts
AML typologies relevant to the bank's customers

name: Customer Advisor
purpose: Delivers an exceptional customer experience as the primary point of contact for customers of the Bank. Handles enquiries, account queries, and service requests while upholding standards for service quality, regulatory compliance, and data protection.
responsibilities: Respond to customer enquiries via phone, email, and digital channels
Resolve customer account queries and product-related issues
Guide customers through onboarding (ID/document collection per KYC requirements)
Recognise and escalate potential fraud or unusual customer behaviour
Log and manage customer complaints per regulatory timeframes
Update customer records accurately per GDPR and data protection policy
Support outbound customer contact for account reviews and document refreshes
key_risk_themes: First point of contact during onboarding — weak ID verification lets illegitimate customers enter
High volume of interactions with limited AML depth increases risk of missing red flags
Reliance on escalation protocols — failure to recognise unusual behaviour breaks chain of defence
Proximity to vulnerable customers — exposure to social engineering tactics

knowledge: AML/KYC fundamentals for customer-facing staff
GDPR and customer data handling
Consumer Duty and complaint handling rules
Tipping-off rules and avoidance
skills: Identify red flags in customer interactions
judgement: Knowing when to escalate without alerting the customer
Balancing customer experience with compliance obligations

name: Money Laundering Reporting Officer (MLRO)
team: Risk and Compliance (Second Line of Defence)
purpose: Provides expert advisory and monitoring service to ensure compliance with rules, regulations and codes of conduct relating to Fraud and Financial Crime. Leads design, development and ongoing monitoring of the Group's Fraud and Financial Crime Risk Management arrangements.
responsibilities: Lead design and review of the Bank's F&FC Risk Management Policies
Provide regular reports to the Chief Risk Officer and Board
Design and facilitate group-wide F&FC risk assessment
Investigate suspected fraud and financial crime cases
Review escalations (Sanctions/PEPs/Adverse media) and approve/decline per policy
Investigate and submit Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) to the Reporting Authority
Design and deliver F&FC training content
Monitor staff compliance with F&FC training requirements
Deputise for the MLRO when required
key_risk_themes: Ultimate accountability for SAR submission decisions — personal criminal liability
Oversees entire financial crime framework — blind spots cascade across all lines of defence
Board-level reporting — inaccurate MI leads to strategic decisions on flawed risk data
External-facing to regulators and law enforcement — highest reputational and enforcement risk

knowledge: Detailed operational knowledge of UK regulatory and legal financial crime requirements
Sanctions regimes and screening obligations
SAR/STR submission process and UKFIU requirements
Board reporting and governance
skills: Translate legislation into clear business-focused procedures
Strategic risk evaluation across organisational structures and products
Quality assurance and thematic review of 1LOD controls
Stakeholder management with regulators and law enforcement
judgement: When to refer to law enforcement (NCA, Action Fraud, SFO)
Resource allocation across F&FC risk priorities

name: Transaction Monitoring Analyst
team: Fraud & Financial Crime / AML Due Diligence and Investigations
purpose: Supports the Bank's anti-money laundering framework by reviewing and investigating transaction monitoring alerts. Assesses whether customer and partner transactions are consistent with known profiles and expected behaviour, escalating cases of concern.
responsibilities: Review transaction monitoring alerts daily against customer risk profiles and expected behaviour
Conduct documented investigations into flagged activity
Determine whether alerts should be discounted, monitored, or escalated as suspicious
Prepare clear investigation notes for audit and compliance
Escalate cases meeting SAR threshold to the nominated officer
Identify emerging typologies in transaction activity
Maintain case management records throughout investigation lifecycle
key_risk_themes: Operates at point where financial crime becomes visible — missed alerts enable layering/integration
High alert volumes increase risk of fatigue-driven errors and incorrect discounting
Inadequate investigation notes weaken audit trail and SAR quality
Delays in escalation can breach regulatory reporting timeframes

knowledge: AML/CTF legislation and UK financial institution obligations
Money laundering typologies and red flag indicators
SAR reporting obligations and UKFIU submission process
Transaction monitoring platforms (e.g. NICE Actimize, Temenos)
skills: Interpret financial transaction data to identify suspicious patterns
Make well-reasoned risk-based decisions under SLA pressure
judgement: Distinguishing legitimate unusual activity from genuine suspicion
When to escalate vs continue monitoring
Managing alert fatigue without compromising quality

name: AML DDI Manager
purpose: Manages AML DDI issues in support of wider due diligence on Product Teams. Responsible for managing all AML/KYC searches against new and existing delivery partners and suppliers, plus coaching analyst-level team members.
responsibilities: Manage KYC/AML search process (new requests, alerts, periodic reviews, closures/exits)
Liaise with BU Directors and Relationship managers on AML DDI processes
Manage the data asset register for GDPR compliance
Operational quality checking and first-line operational risk compliance
Coach, mentor and develop analyst-level team members
Provide cover for Senior Manager during absence
key_risk_themes: Manages AML/KYC checks on delivery partners — third-party risk is a known ML vulnerability
Oversight failures in periodic reviews can allow non-compliant partners to remain active
GDPR mismanagement of data asset register creates dual regulatory exposure
Coaching and QA responsibilities mean individual errors propagate across analyst team

knowledge: AML and KYC across financial services
Third-party / delivery partner risk frameworks
GDPR data asset register requirements
Operational risk and SLA management
judgement: Resource and priority management across team caseload
